text: The surgeon incises the peritoneum along the superior and inferior borders of the pancreas, mobilizing the pancreatic neck. The superior mesenteric vein and portal vein confluence are identified retro-pancreatically. Once the pancreas is fully mobilized, the proximal pancreas is transected using a stapler, followed by the distal pancreas, which is transected with an ultrasonic device. The pancreatic duct is transected with scissors, and the splenic vessels are preserved.

The surgeon drains the distal pancreas into the jejunum, constructing an end-to-side pancreaticojejunostomy. Layers are formed with interrupted sutures, using 4-0 Prolene to secure the posterior row between the jejunal seromuscular layer and the pancreatic capsule.
The surgeon creates a small anastomosis opposite the pancreatic duct on the wall of the jejunum, using interrupted sutures of 5-0 prolene to approximate the tissues.

A small-sized silicon catheter is placed through the anastomosis into the pancreatic duct and exits through a tunnel in the jejunal lobe. The anastomosis is completed with an anterior row of four-zero proline sutures, securing the pancreatic capsule to the seromuscular layers of the jejunum.
